title: 牙医谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\203-牙医谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot reviews his notes concerning several individuals: Ambrosio, a potential communist spy; Frank Carter, a recently unemployed man; Howard Ricks; and Mr. Barnes. He also considers potential carpet traces found at Moor's office. Poirot travels to 88 Castle Lane to visit Mr. Barnes, a retired Civil Service official.

During their meeting, Mr. Barnes reveals that he recognized Ambrosio in a waiting room and believes Ambrosio belongs to a group of foreign agitators. According to Mr. Barnes, these agitators aim to destroy the British financial system by eliminating Alistair Bret, a man responsible for maintaining strict financial stability. Mr. Barnes deduces that this group uses "natural" accidents to remove influential figures, citing three previous examples: a Chancellor of the Exchequer who died from an anesthetist's mistake, an entrepreneur who died of an undiagnosed illness, and a politician who died in a car accident. In each instance, the professionals involved—the anesthetist, the physician, and the driver—subsequently achieved significant wealth and comfort.

Mr. Barnes argues that while the dentist, Morley, may not be the mastermind, he was likely eliminated because he knew too much. He suggests an organization is behind a plot to kill Bret and speculates that Layton is the most likely person to perform the deed. Mr. Barnes proposes a scheme where Molly would provide an excuse for being unwell at the last minute, allowing Layton to perform the task. This would result in a "medical accident" that leaves the dentist appearing depressed and pitiful in court, eventually allowing him to retire with a large income.
